evals.expected_output: ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW.md written next to the codebase root and shown inline, reporting the a.py/b.py cycle as a high-confidence Circular dependencies finding with the concrete risk explained and a suggested fix (e.g. extracting a shared interface).
assertions: ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW.md is written next to the codebase root and also shown inline
The cycle between pkg/a.py and pkg/b.py is reported with confidence High
The finding explains a concrete risk (e.g. init-order bugs, tight coupling), not just a restatement of the cycle

evals.expected_output: A structural review identifying the data-layer-importing-from-handlers layering violation, with severity/confidence and a suggested fix.
assertions: A layering violation finding referencing data/repository.py and handlers/http.py is present
No architecture pattern is invented for the codebase to be judged against unless the codebase itself declares one
Findings are grouped by severity with a scan-coverage section noting which languages had a dependency graph built
